title: Status date is "292 years ago"
body:
### Issue Description

I have the same issue as previously reported in issue #25122. 

My stopped containers show `Exited (0) 292 years ago` 



### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. Start containers
2. Stop containers
3. Run `podman ps`


### Describe the results you received


```
podman ps -a
CONTAINER ID  IMAGE                                           COMMAND               CREATED      STATUS                    PORTS                                               NAMES
e1b83af5a98e  docker.io/datalust/seq:latest                                         4 hours ago  Exited (0) 292 years ago  0.0.0.0:5341->80/tcp, 443/tcp, 5341/tcp, 45341/tcp  seq
29e1422fc208  mcr.microsoft.com/mssql/server:2022-latest      /opt/mssql/bin/sq...  4 hours ago  Exited (0) 292 years ago  0.0.0.0:1433->1433/tcp                              db
ce2d7ec3e4ec  docker.io/redis/redis-stack:latest              /entrypoint.sh        4 hours ago  Exited (0) 292 years ago  0.0.0.0:6379->6379/tcp, 0.0.0.0:8001->8001/tcp      redis
30fe366b4320  mcr.microsoft.com/azure-storage/azurite:latest  azurite -l /data ...  4 hours ago  Exited (0) 292 years ago  0.0.0.0:10000-10002->10000-10002/tcp                azurite
```

### Describe the results you expected

Describe the results you expected

```
podman ps -a
CONTAINER ID  IMAGE                                           COMMAND               CREATED      STATUS                    PORTS                                               NAMES
e1b83af5a98e  docker.io/datalust/seq:latest                                         4 hours ago  Exited (0) 2 minutes ago  0.0.0.0:5341->80/tcp, 443/tcp, 5341/tcp, 45341/tcp  seq
29e1422fc208  mcr.microsoft.com/mssql/server:2022-latest      /opt/mssql/bin/sq...  4 hours ago  Exited (0) 2 minutes  ago  0.0.0.0:1433->1433/tcp                              db
ce2d7ec3e4ec  docker.io/redis/redis-stack:latest              /entrypoint.sh        4 hours ago  Exited (0) 2 minutes ago  0.0.0.0:6379->6379/tcp, 0.0.0.0:8001->8001/tcp      redis
30fe366b4320  mcr.microsoft.com/azure-storage/azurite:latest  azurite -l /data ...  4 hours ago  Exited (0) 2 minutes ago  0.0.0.0:10000-10002->10000-10002/tcp                azurite
```

### Privileged Or Rootless

### Upstream Latest Release

### Additional environment details

Additional environment details

### Additional information

This happens always. I use Swedish for input and formats but English for text.

comments.body:
@yooakim are you able to find a reproducer for us? I think that was the problem in the original issue reported by @mathstuf.  were the containers running for long periods of time or were they interupted ungracefully?

@mheon would anything from the DB tell us what might be happening ?
while you are on windows, it looks like you are not running in a podman machine? is that correct? and it looks like podman might be running in a fedora container ?
We source the timestamps from the filesystem (created time for the exit file), which makes me wonder if Podman is running its tmpdir on a filesystem with wierd timestamps?

> We source the timestamps from the filesystem (created time for the exit file), which makes me wonder if Podman is running its tmpdir on a filesystem with wierd timestamps?

I don't think that is is btw, what is much more likely is 292 years ago matches what you would get with the zero type for `time.Time`

292 years ago is the max value of the golang duration AFAIK, based on our client code which renders in "human readable"
```golang
package main

import (
	"fmt"
	"time"
)

func main() {
	d := time.Since(time.Time{})
	fmt.Println(int64(d.Hours()) / 24 / 365)
}

```
https://go.dev/play/p/3pJnnFedwwq
https://pkg.go.dev/time#Duration 

So likely on some code path state.FinishedTime stays unset, so yeah it would nice to get a proper reproducer for this to figure out the code path when it happens.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
Still needs investigation. I'd let it expire, but it is evidently more than a "just me" problem.
Well we will need a proper reproducer to work with.
I suspect it has to do with ungraceful shutdowns while the container is running.
Sorry I've been off-line for some time. I can try to help reproduce if it helps? The containers were not long running so probably not related to that. @baude

I was able to reproduce this issue, please follow below steps:

  * Keep multiple containers in `running` state.
  * Fill the host filesystem completely (e.g. using `fallocate -l 20G /testfile6`) so it hits full disk.
  * While still full, `podman ps or podman ps -a` shows:

    * `Error: adding container 8173ae7b83453646757f73cde5ccd165306db2bd0ccfdc39fb5001eb6192ae42 exit code 127: database or disk is full`
    * `ERRO[0000] Rolling back transaction to add exit code: cannot rollback - no transaction is active`
  * Reboot the machine.
  * Free disk space by `rm -rf /testfile6`.
  * Run `podman ps -a` → affected containers show:

    * `Exited (...) 292 years ago`

Also reproduced from my side:

```
$ podman ps -a|grep debian-sid
94ae1ffa444d  docker.io/library/debian:sid        --verbose --name ...  8 months ago   Exited (0) 292 years ago                debian-sid
$ podman inspect debian-sid |grep -i finish
               "FinishedAt": "0001-01-01T00:00:00Z",
```
Is your disk also full/nearly full?
